P0: web browser acceptance when Web user-visible behavior changes
P1: affected module, integration, system, API, data-flow, or cross-boundary checks
risk assessment when a P1 check is skipped
P2: broader regression, repeated runs, cross-browser or mobile viewport, accessibility, or performance hardening
explicit defer reason when not run

required_evidence: screenshot, trace, video, log, or explicit skip reason
web_acceptance_policy.default_tool_boundary: Use webapp-testing for one-off local acceptance; use e2e-testing only for durable Playwright suites.
web_acceptance_policy.safety_boundary: Do not use production accounts, real payments, or external writes unless explicitly approved.

actionable_resolution: fix failing checks that are within the accepted task scope
pr_closeout_policy.merge_boundary: Do not merge a PR unless the user explicitly requests that remote mutation.

required_checks: final independent review or explicit skip reason
technical debt and first-principles implementation fit
PR or merge-readiness status when a PR exists
conflict decisions and risk surfaced to the user
insight audit recommendations or explicit skip reason
finish_closeout_policy.subagent_boundary: Use subagents only for independent read-only review or verification; the main agent owns synthesis and final decisions.
finish_closeout_policy.insight_boundary: Use insight to audit tool calling, repeated low-value lookup loops, misleading evidence, docs/code conflicts, AI infrastructure lessons, and skill or workflow extraction candidates.
finish_closeout_policy.merge_boundary: Drive to merge-ready or complete the merge only when the user explicitly authorizes that remote mutation.

agentic_loop_policy.loop_model: Producer -> Verifier -> Arbiter -> Main Agent Decision

runtime_state.write_policy: Subagents write private runtime state and leased product paths only; the main agent summarizes accepted evidence into progress and handoff state.

required_evidence: verifier evidence or explicit skip reason
arbiter verdict or explicit skip reason
stop condition when a loop may repeat or iteration controls are recorded
iteration and maxIterations when a loop may repeat

rules: single-pass loops may omit iteration, maxIterations, and stopCondition
when either iteration field is present, both values must be positive integers and stopCondition is required
iteration must be less than or equal to maxIterations
allowed stopCondition values are continue, revise, interrupt, deliver, handoff, and complete

agentic_loop_policy.arbiter_boundary: Arbiters are read-only. They do not edit code, resolve conflicts, push, publish, merge, or make final user-facing decisions.
agentic_loop_policy.lease_boundary: Write-capable delegated agents may share the current worktree only after a path lease records non-overlapping owned paths; dirty-scope changes outside owned paths block integration.
agentic_loop_policy.dispatch_boundary: Hooks and deterministic checks may request or validate loop evidence, but must not auto-dispatch delegated agents.
agentic_loop_policy.main_agent_responsibility: The main agent owns synthesis, user-visible decisions, integration, and final handoff.

loop_control_policy.control_plane: Loop Engineering decides when to continue, validate, interrupt, and record auditable action decisions.
loop_control_policy.default_behavior: Continue low-risk local work when scope, ownership, and validation are clear; interrupt when risk signals require human review.

context_engineering_policy.knowledge_layer: LLM Wiki keeps stable project knowledge in Markdown without duplicating raw sources or agent-native memory.
context_engineering_policy.write_policy: Initialize incrementally; require human confirmation before knowledge writes; preserve wiki content file-by-file during standard updates.

worktree_policy.write_task_isolation: Use one git worktree or branch per write task.
worktree_policy.code_organization: Keep source code organized by project architecture, not by agent task folders.
